summary: Two important findings: ANSI padding bug in show_summary() corrupts the final box frame (game.py:54), and missing stdout encoding guard causes UnicodeEncodeError traceback on non-UTF-8 terminals (game.py:276). Three minor findings: misleading comment with unused variable, unused import, missing .gitignore entry. All 7 task verification commands pass; implementation is otherwise correct, stdlib-only, and scope-clean.

findings.description: print_box() calculates padding via len(wline) which counts ANSI escape bytes. show_summary() passes lines containing ANSI color codes, making padding 9+ bytes too short per line — the right box wall renders out of position on the final summary screen.
findings.evidence: len('\033[32m\u2713\033[0m  1 · Brief') == 31, visible length == 12, difference == 9. Confirmed by measurement script run against all 7 phase lines.
findings.suggestion: In print_box() line 54, compute visible length via re.sub to strip ANSI: visible = len(re.sub(r'\x1b\[[0-9;]*m', '', wline)); use visible instead of len(wline) for padding.

findings.description: No sys.stdout.reconfigure() or PYTHONIOENCODING guard in game.py. On cp1252 terminals the game aborts with a raw UnicodeEncodeError traceback instead of a readable error message.
findings.evidence: sys.stdout.encoding confirmed as cp1252 on this machine. Check script produced: UnicodeEncodeError: 'charmap' codec can't encode character '\u2500' in position 23: character maps to <undefined>
findings.suggestion: Add sys.stdout.reconfigure(encoding='utf-8', errors='replace') as first statement in main() for graceful fallback on non-UTF-8 terminals.

findings.description: Comment on line 43 claims ANSI bytes are excluded from centering. Variable 'clean' is assigned but never used; line.center(w) is called on the raw 'line' with ANSI bytes intact on line 44.
findings.evidence: Line 43: clean = line  # Zentrieren auf sichtbare Breite (ohne ANSI-Bytes). Line 44: print(color + line.center(w) + RESET). 'clean' is never referenced.
findings.suggestion: Remove the misleading comment and unused 'clean' variable, or implement actual ANSI stripping.

findings.description: import sys at game.py:7 is unused. AST walk found no sys.* attribute access anywhere in the file.
findings.evidence: AST name-usage analysis: sys absent from all Attribute nodes. Bash check output: 'sys benutzt: False'
findings.suggestion: Remove 'import sys' — or retain if F-002 fix is adopted since sys.stdout.reconfigure requires sys.

findings.description: .sdd_game_seen marker file is not listed in .gitignore. The file is already physically present in the project root. In a shared repo, committing it sets the skip-intro flag for all clones.
findings.evidence: Full .gitignore read: '.sdd_game_seen' not present in any line. ls output confirms .sdd_game_seen exists in project root.

verdict: FAIL — two important findings require fixes before quality-complete. F-001 breaks visual output of the final summary screen. F-002 causes an unhandled crash with no user-readable error on non-UTF-8 terminals. Minor findings F-003 through F-005 can be addressed in a follow-up pass.

game.py:109 'Vibe-Coding', game.py:138 SDD contrast narrative confirmed in source
game.py:291-292 — main() loops over all 7 PHASES via run_phase()
game.py:221-233 — run_phase() renders name, zweck, kernfrage, prompt for each phase
AST walk: no subprocess import, no os.system('sdd') call found
Imports: os, sys, shutil, textwrap — all Python stdlib confirmed by AST
Works on Windows Terminal (UTF-8) and Unix. F-002 documents graceful-degradation gap on cmd.exe, which is out-of-scope per README.
requirements_checked.notes: AC-005 specifies Windows Terminal, not cmd.exe

game.py:240-257 — show_summary() lists all 7 phase names from PHASES
game.py:287-293 — show_intro() called before phase loop in main()

AST walk: no .features/ path construction, no subprocess or sdd commands
game.py:280-285 — input prompt shown when marker exists
Only stdlib imports confirmed by AST import-node analysis

Correct: skip-check -> show_intro -> phases loop -> show_summary
